mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers immediate risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or t-stat for return difference versus baseline is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales and housing starts signals broad consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects accommodative policy transmission to real output, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing periods produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations, or regime-conditional IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly GDP growth indicates stronger aggregate demand, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations adjust.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day returns with Rank IC below 0.005 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 235.0, anticipated central bank tightening compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 70 percent of periods where CPIAUCSL crosses above 235.0, or inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment signals labor market tightening, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from independent main effects.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real sales during a zero-lower-bound monetary regime indicate demand collapse unmitigated by policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during ZLB periods generate positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances, or regime-specific predictive accuracy falls below random chance.
